serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of lemon sandwich biscuits is approximately 30 g, equivalent to about 3 individual sandwich biscuit units as commonly sold in snack packs.

nutrient_source.title: Sandwich Biscuit (Lemon Filling) — HPB Singapore Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sandwich Biscuit (Lemon Filling), CR ID F11080056. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Biscuits and Cookies — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for lemon-filled sandwich biscuit based on plain sweet biscuit GI reference (GI ~70)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for lemon-filled sandwich biscuit in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 (high) is estimated from the closest analogous food: plain sweet biscuit / cream-filled sandwich cookie category entries in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 65–75. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (70 × 19.0) / 100 = 13.3, based on 30 g serving containing approximately 19.0 g available carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (423 mg/100 g) combined with high sugar (24.79 g/100 g) and very low dietary fibre (0.8 g/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid lemon sandwich biscuits; sodium at 423 mg/100 g approaches the high-sodium threshold, and the high sugar content (24.79 g/100 g) with negligible fibre (0.8 g/100 g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention, compounding blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (24.79 g/100 g), high refined carbohydrate (63.37 g/100 g), very low dietary fibre (0.8 g/100 g), and high GI (~70)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid lemon sandwich biscuits entirely; the combination of high GI (~70), very high sugar (24.79 g/100 g exceeding the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling), and minimal fibre (0.8 g/100 g) will cause rapid and significant post-prandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (25.19 g/100 g) with estimated high saturated fat (~12 g/100 g from palm oil), high sodium (423 mg/100 g), and high sugar (24.79 g/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid lemon sandwich biscuits; the high saturated fat content (~12 g/100 g, predominantly from palm oil), elevated sodium (423 mg/100 g), and high sugar (24.79 g/100 g) collectively raise LDL cholesterol, blood pressure, and triglyceride levels, all of which are independent cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar (24.79 g/100 g) and high saturated fat (~12 g/100 g from palm oil) promote systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid lemon sandwich biscuits; the high refined sugar and palm oil-derived saturated fat content promote pro-inflammatory mediators that may exacerbate joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sandwich Biscuit (Lemon Filling) is an ultra-processed sweet snack consisting of two thin, crisp baked biscuit wafers sandwiching a lemon-flavoured cream filling. The biscuit shells are made from refined wheat flour, sugar, vegetable fat (typically palm oil), salt, and raising agents, while the filling is a sweet, artificially lemon-flavoured cream composed primarily of sugar, vegetable shortening, and flavouring agents. The texture is crisp and crumbly on the outside with a soft, sweet, and tangy cream centre. This product is widely available in Singapore school canteens and convenience stores as a popular snack item. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 509 kcal per 100 g, with high total fat (25.19 g/100 g, predominantly saturated from palm oil at an estimated 12 g/100 g), very high sugar (24.79 g/100 g), high refined carbohydrate (63.37 g/100 g), and elevated sodium (423 mg/100 g). Dietary fibre is negligible at 0.8 g/100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 70 (high), making this a high-glycaemic food with rapid glucose absorption. This product is clinically unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar, high saturated fat, high sodium, and pro-inflammatory nutritional profile. It should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions by healthy individuals.

How many calories in a lemon sandwich biscuit?

Are sandwich biscuits suitable for heart patients?
How much sodium is in lemon cream biscuits?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11080056) with confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on the closest equivalent sweet biscuit category, reducing overall GI confidence but not the nutrient confidence. Saturated fat estimated at ~12 g/100 g based on typical palm oil composition for this biscuit category; monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat data not available in the HPB source record.
notes: 1. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 12 g/100 g based on typical palm oil fatty acid composition for this biscuit category; this value is not directly reported in the HPB source and should be verified against product packaging when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this processed biscuit product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F11080056 as of 2026. 3. iron_mg is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source; this is a confirmed zero per the HPB database record — refined wheat flour and palm oil-based biscuits typically contain negligible iron. 4. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on plain sweet biscuit and cream-filled sandwich cookie category entries (GI range 65–75); no direct GI measurement for lemon-filled sandwich biscuit was found. 5. Halal status not confirmed from available data; product may be halal-certified depending on manufacturer — verify against product packaging. 6. This food is classified as ultra-processed (NOVA Group 4) due to the use of refined ingredients, artificial flavouring, and industrial processing.
